analyses.description: Brain activation in fMRI (BOLD contrast) elicited by alcohol-associated cues compared with abstract pictures in six alcohol-dependent patients. In the table, x, y, z represent coordinates in the stereotactic atlas of Talairach and Tournaux; statistical significance of the activation is given as Student’s t-value (SPM99, fixed effects model). We used a cluster threshold of 10 voxels.

analyses.description: Brain areas in alcohol-dependent patients in which decrease of fMRI cue reactivity (alcohol − neutral cues, T1 − T2) was larger after 3 weeks of cue-exposure treatment (n = 15) compared with standard clinical treatment (n = 15), p < .001 uncorrected; cluster size ≥10 voxels. CET, cue-exposure based extinction training; fMRI, functional magnetic resonance imaging; FWE, family-wise error rate; MNI, Montreal Neurological Institute; SV, small volume.
